ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Vietnamese dried fish mango salad; 'salad food product' (FOODON_00002809) used as the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fish and fish products — mixed fish dishes
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (mixed fish dishes) used as the closest defensible category; no specific code exists for Vietnamese dried fish mango salad.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of dried fish mango salad is approximately 150 g, presented as a shared appetiser or individual starter plate.

nutrient_source.title: Gỏi Xoài Khô Sặc — Dried Fish Mango Salad
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Gỏi Xoài Khô Sặc (Dried Fish Mango Salad), CR ID G00010575, confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated at 7.0 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central data for fried shallots, peanuts, and dried fish components proportionally weighted. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water content not reported by HPB source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — green mango and mixed salad analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Gỏi Xoài Khô Sặc. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the dominant carbohydrate contributor being green (unripe) mango, which has a published GI of approximately 41–51 in the University of Sydney GI Database. Dried fish, fish sauce, fried shallots, and peanuts contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially raise the dish GI. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (45 × 8.85) / 100 ≈ 4.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; driven by dried salted fish and fish sauce dressing
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request reduced fish sauce, rinse dried fish before use, and limit portion to no more than 50 g to keep sodium intake below 405 mg per eating occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 5.9 g/100 g with estimated low GI ~45 from green mango; sugar 4.69 g/100 g is below the 15 g/100 g threshold
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management in moderate portions; use green (unripe) mango only, request reduced sugar in the dressing, and monitor total caloric intake given the high fat content of 44.5 g/100 g from peanuts and fried shallots.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat 44.5 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat ~7.0 g/100 g from fried shallots and peanuts; sodium 810 mg/100 g compounds cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to high saturated fat and sodium content; if consumed, request preparation with reduced fried shallots, minimal fish sauce, and limit portion to 50–75 g to reduce saturated fat and sodium load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100 g worsens tissue oedema; chili (nightshade) may trigger joint inflammation; saturated fat ~7.0 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish due to high sodium, nightshade chili content, and elevated saturated fat; if consumed, request chili-free preparation, reduced fish sauce, and limit portion to 50 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dried Fish Mango Salad (Gỏi Xoài Khô Sặc) is a Vietnamese salad dish featuring shredded green (unripe) mango tossed with dried featherback fish (khô sặc), a salted and sun-dried freshwater fish prized for its intense umami flavour. The salad is dressed with a tangy-sweet-spicy mixture of fish sauce, lime juice, sugar, and fresh chili, then garnished with fried shallots, crushed roasted peanuts, and fresh Vietnamese herbs such as rau răm (Vietnamese coriander). The dish delivers a complex interplay of sour, sweet, salty, and spicy flavours with contrasting textures — crisp green mango, crunchy peanuts, and chewy dried fish. Nutritionally, the dish is calorie-dense at 430 kcal/100 g, driven primarily by high fat content (44.5 g/100 g) from peanuts and fried shallots. Protein is low at 1.32 g/100 g and carbohydrate is low at 5.9 g/100 g, making it suitable for glycaemic management when portion-controlled. Sodium is very high at 810 mg/100 g due to the salted dried fish and fish sauce, making it unsuitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients without significant modification. The dish is popular in Vietnamese restaurants in Singapore and is typically served as a shared appetiser or starter. Clinically, it is flagged high concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to sodium and saturated fat, but low concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to low carbohydrate and low GI from green mango.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID G00010575) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for constituent ingredients. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (green mango). All mandatory Singaporean and Vietnamese language fields populated.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB source; this is plausible given the small quantity of green mango shreds relative to the high-fat dressing and dried fish components, and is accepted as confirmed zero per HPB. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and moisture data for this specific Vietnamese salad preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient panel not reported by HPB for this dish entry. saturated_fat_g of 7.0 g/100g is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central component data for fried shallots, roasted peanuts, and dried fish proportionally weighted to the dish composition; this value should be confirmed when a direct HPB or ICMR-NIN measurement becomes available. The dish name variant 'Khô Xặc' (with diacritic xặc) appears in the input record name; 'Khô Sặc' (sặc) is the standard Vietnamese spelling for the featherback fish species and is used as the primary Vietnamese name.
